CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, name String, data Array(Int32));
SELECT * FROM postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'test_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'A%');
SELECT name FROM postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'test_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') WHERE data[1] >= 2;
CREATE NAMED COLLECTION mypg AS host = 'localhost', port = 5432, database = 'test', user = 'postgresql_user', password = 'password';
SELECT * FROM postgresql(mypg, table='test_table') WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE arrayJoin(data) > 5);
CREATE TABLE another_table (user_id UInt32, score Float64);

SELECT t1.name, t2.score FROM postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'test_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') t1 JOIN postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'another_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') t2 ON t1.id = t2.user_id WHERE t2.score > (SELECT AVG(score) FROM another_table);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'test_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') WHERE data[1] IS NOT NULL AND data[2] IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'test_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') WHERE arrayJoin(data) IN (1, 4, 7);
SELECT name FROM postgresql('localhost:5432', 'test', 'test_table', 'postgresql_user', 'password') WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM another_table WHERE score > 12);
